pi_items.verified_value: Official Wireshark sample capture file (http.cap) from https://wiki.wireshark.org/uploads/27707187aeb30df68e70c8fb9d614981/http.cap
pi_items.source: Downloaded and verified from official Wireshark wiki repository; confirmed by install_wireshark.sh script

pi_items.verified_value: A simple HTTP request and response capture containing TCP handshake, HTTP GET request, and HTTP 200 OK response
pi_items.source: Analyzed via tshark; verified packet structure contains frames 1-2 (SYN/SYN-ACK), frame 4 (GET), frame 27 (200 OK)
Calculated from http.cap: timestamp of SYN-ACK (1084443428.222534000) minus timestamp of SYN (1084443427.311224000)
Calculated from http.cap: timestamp of HTTP 200 OK (1084443431.266912000) minus timestamp of HTTP GET request (1084443428.222534000)
Matches task.json metadata; appropriate for latency measurements in the 900ms-3000ms range; represents 1 millisecond tolerance

pi_items.verified_value: Official Wireshark project sample captures repository
pi_items.source: Confirmed via install_wireshark.sh (lines 80-83) which downloads from wiki.wireshark.org and gitlab.com/wireshark mirrors
Counted via tshark analysis of downloaded http.cap file

privileged_info_summary: The task uses the official Wireshark http.cap sample capture file (real, non-synthetic data from wiki.wireshark.org). The capture contains a TCP handshake followed by an HTTP GET/200 OK exchange. Network latency (TCP SYN to SYN-ACK) measures 0.911310 seconds; server processing latency (HTTP GET to 200 OK) measures 3.044378 seconds. The tolerance threshold is 0.001 seconds (1 ms). Data is verifiable, publicly available, and consistent across the install scripts and task specifications.
